title: Refactor function-head soup with multi-clause dispatch
prompt: Write lib/my_app_web/live/settings_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.SettingsLive). The handle_event("validate") must use a single clause with a tiny discriminating head `handle_event("validate", %{"settings" => params}, socket)` — no field destructuring in the head, no binding of assigns in the head. Field-level logic lives in the body via `Enum.reduce` over params or similar. Also write a second clause `handle_event("save", %{"settings" => params}, socket)` for submission. The fixture test_fixtures/function_head_soup.ex shows the anti-pattern.
tier_rationale: Requires the cleanup of both the original soup AND the additional save clause. Consistent application of the rule.
research_citation: John Elm Labs anti-patterns: 'Function Head Pattern Matching Abuse'
